description: A consumer applies each event without deduping, so at-least-once redelivery double-applies the effect.
body: Failure mode: the consumer processes an event and commits the offset assuming the broker delivered it once, but after a crash between effect and commit the event is redelivered and re-applied. Detection signals: no processed-id check or idempotent upsert; offset committed before the effect is durable; duplicate downstream rows/charges after consumer restarts. Do not trust broker 'exactly-once' for external side effects. Safer fallback: dedup on the event id atomically with the effect, commit the offset only after the effect commits, and add a redelivery test.
